programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Jobless is a sitcom all about the Adenugas, a middle-class British Nigerian family in Milton Keynes, in particular 16-year-old twin brother and sister Kehinde and Taiwo. Kenny is the family joker, who dreams of fame and fortune; sister Tai is, by contrast, sarcastic, super-smart and anxious to prove it. The twins live at home with their parents. Their mum, Bunmi, (chatty, energetic, control-freak tendencies, has a very stern side) runs Bunmi’s Beauty Salon, and despairs at her hapless offspring and their schemes to hit the big time. Tai and Kenny are desperate to make money by any means necessary – not only is financial independence very alluring, but the pressure they face from Bunmi doesn’t help. The twins’ different personalities inform their different approaches, however it seems neither of those personalities are quite compatible with the world of work.

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Live international football as Wales welcome World Cup-bound Ghana to the Cardiff City Stadium.

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: Louis reflects on the criminal justice system in the USA, which has more prisoners than any other country in the world. In documentaries such as Behind Bars and Miami Mega Jail, Louis spent time with hundreds of incarcerated men, discovering a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. In A Place for Paedophiles, Louis came face to face with those responsible for serious sex crimes against children who were being locked up indefinitely, even after their sentences had been completed. Louis also revisits his documentaries in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point, years before the recent killing of George Floyd.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May and the Stig return. Jeremy asks why there aren't more three-wheeled cars in the world and investigates further with a closer look at the 1980s comedians' favourite, the Reliant Robin. Meanwhile, James gives Top Gear's favourite warhorse, the Toyota Hilux pick up, one final assignment as he attempts to drive one up an active volcano in Iceland and the Stig rips up the track in the new Bentley Continental Supersports. Plus, Richard gives the old Chevrolet Lacetti a dignified send-off before he and Clarkson host a star-studded tea party to welcome in a shiny new Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the death of a chef in an arson attack on his restaurant, John and Janie come to a small fishing village to scrutinise the locals.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. Haunted by his own penalty miss with England, he inherits a broken team and a jaded fanbase. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team’s mindset and tackling his and England’s biggest fear – penalties.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. As spring gives way to summer, wildlife is at its most active and visitor numbers rise, but wildfires pose a growing threat. They meet the people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK’s seaweed farming industry.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Some more unusual methods of growing are demonstrated at Beechgrove Garden. Ruth Vichos attempts to produce a mushroom crop and there’s a report on some of Scotland’s best bonsai, with everything you need to know to start that form of cultivation. Brian Cunningham is in the Seaside Garden at Beechgrove refreshing the planting with varieties that can withstand a coastal climate and Brian turns conifers into sculptures with topiary. Plus the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie visits Bee Edge Farm near Eyemouth to help open the barn doors and let the cows out for summer. Continuing to mark Landward’s 50th anniversary, Cammy catches up with a farming pioneer who was never one for sitting on the fence, and Rosie is stepping back in time in Aberdeenshire to find out what one old farm building can tell us about our agricultural past. Elsewhere, Leanna is on the trail of a walrus that has been turning up in harbours around Scotland's north coast.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Imad Alarnab brings a taste of Syria to the studio with his Middle Eastern feast – mujadara, pickled cucumbers, laban bikhiar and baba ganoush. Shivi Ramoutar has the perfect recipe for a fried fish taco, finished with a punchy green mango chow and tamarind pepper sauce. Patrick Williams serves the flavours of South Africa with his peri-peri poussin with confit potatoes, while Jacob Kenedy prepares two simple but tasty granitas. Pastry perfectionist Ravneet Gill rustles up a delightful summertime strawberry and custard tart, and Laura Smyth faces either her food heaven or her food hell. Meanwhile, Rick Stein takes to the shores of Cornwall to forage for razor clams, and the Hairy Bikers head to Tewkesbury to learn all about mustard balls.

programme.title: Athletics: Great Manchester Run
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 2026 Great Manchester Run.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Nigel Slater creates simple but delicious meals from ingredients that are often overlooked, including a lentil and chard stew.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook and Share
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry goes behind the scenes at a huge wedding, sharing memories of her own special day and cooking up some delicious dishes perfect for sharing at special occasions.

programme.title: Operation Crossbow
programme.desc: World War II drama. A small group of agents is sent on a vital mission to destroy a Nazi V-2 rocket base in Holland. Their aim is to prevent flying bombs from falling on London.

programme.title: The Searchers
programme.desc: Classic John Ford western. Believing the Comanche have abducted his young niece, embittered Ethan Edwards sets out to save her, accompanied by hot-headed Martin. But their long quest takes its toll.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan to find one of the world’s most endangered big cats – the snow leopard. Kyrgyzstan sits at the heart of the global snow leopard population, but from the Himalayas in the south to Russia in the north, snow leopards are under threat right across their range. The goal of this expedition is to explore the remote and impenetrable Djangart valley. Snow leopards are known to live north and south of here, but nothing is known about what lies between. No one has studied the cats here because these high-altitude peaks are so difficult to access, but this region is packed with precious minerals, and mining companies now have their sights firmly set on the Djangart valley. Teaming up with Kyrgyz conservation group Snow Leopard Foundation, Steve and the team set out to find evidence of a breeding population of snow leopards. If they can find snow leopards living and breeding here, they hope to persuade the government of Kyrgyzstan to turn the Djangart valley into a protected nature reserve.

programme.title: Southbank at 75: You Are Here
programme.desc: An epic dance spectacular and electrifying journey through 75 years of British youth culture to mark Southbank Centre’s landmark birthday. This is a freewheeling journey of discovery across decades, remixing and reimagining some of the most vivid and influential youth and social movements that have driven culture forward since 1951, when the south bank of the Thames hosted the Festival of Britain.

programme.title: Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: Coverage from the 2026 Diamond League meeting at Rabat, Morocco.

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: In 2010, poorly 16-year-old Megan Bhari decides to launch a charity for seriously ill children. She’s battling a brain tumour, and knowing the toll illness can take, she decides to devote her life to creating fairy-tale experiences that might help children to forget, even briefly, that they’re sick. Believe in Magic becomes an immediate success. Inspired by Megan’s courage and self-sacrifice, celebrities and her beloved One Direction are more than willing to donate their time and money to the cause. And when her mother, Jean, posts news that Megan’s health has deteriorated and she needs expensive treatment in America, countless families and celebrity supporters rally around to support Meg. Together, they raise the money needed overnight. However, in an online chat, some parents whose own children have been treated for cancer abroad begin asking questions.

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes vocal powerhouse Jessie Ware, pop star Holly Humberstone, prolific producer Jack Antonoff, soul singer Samm Henshaw, electro duo Fcukers and director Tim Pope.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers
programme.desc: Jake Blues rejoins his brother Elwood after being released from prison, but the duo have just days to reunite their old R&B band and save the Catholic home where the two were raised, outrunning the police as they tear through Chicago.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special episode devoted to one of the most significant and consequential issues of our age: artificial intelligence. On the panel are: from the government, Darren Jones, chief secretary to the prime minister; Julia Lopez, shadow secretary of state for science, innovation and technology; Mo Gawdat, author, entrepreneur and former chief business officer at Google X; Laura Gilbert, senior director of AI at the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change; and Victor Riparbelli, founder and CEO of London-based AI company Synthesia.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: As Saudi Arabia opens up to the outside world, miles and miles of its vast desert landscape remain completely unknown. Over 120,000 years ago, the Arabian desert was a pathway for the first human migrations out of Africa. On this expedition, Steve and the team are following in ancient footsteps to shine a light into the unknown. Joining forces with Saudi Arabian geologists Mahmoud Al-Shanti and Hala Alwagdani, Steve and ropes expert Aldo Kane head down into an unexplored volcanic underworld in search of the longest lava tube in Arabia. Geological evidence suggests that at least 400km of tunnels lie beneath the Harrat Khaybar lava field – subterranean passageways which could contain bones and artefacts from ancient civilisations. Only 25km of these lava tubes have ever been explored - until now. Steve and the team start their journey at the ancient Nabataean city of Hegra, where secrets lie waiting to be uncovered.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2010. Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: After a breakthrough World Cup reignites belief, the England team return to a tournament on home turf, amidst the Covid pandemic. As the pressure to win increases, Pippa Grange questions her role. Meanwhile, Gareth tries to unite a divided country ahead of Euro 2020, where he hopes the team finally bring it home at Wembley.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion visits a pub frozen in time, where last orders were called in the 1960s and everything was left untouched. In the Devon town of Buckfastleigh, the Valiant Soldier was an inn for more than two centuries and is now a museum, giving visitors a glimpse into the past. Inside the recreated interior, Christina is told about the pub’s redoubtable landlady and the custom back in the 60s of refusing service to women in the bar. In Hele, near Exeter, two teams of shoppers rush around an antiques centre with their experts, hoping to bag profitable bargains. On the red team are dad and daughter Ade and Mel, helped by Ben Cooper, and for the blues, it’s friends Martin and Mark, aided by Gary Pe. With 60 minutes on the clock, the blue team’s first stop is a shining suit of armour. Across the centre, Mel gazes into a £200 crystal ball – does it foretell a win for the reds? In Chippenham, auctioneer Tim Weeks confidently predicts a few profits for the teams, but who will go home the victor?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Before boarding his train at Budapest’s historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth. Heading south, his next destination is the country’s third city of Szeged, close to the Serbian and Romanian borders. In the city’s historic centre, he finds the magnificent Great Synagogue, built in 1902, which reflects the size and prominence of Szeged’s Jewish population at that time. Just outside the city, on the banks of the River Tisza, Michael visits a region known for producing Hungary’s favourite spice, paprika, from ground red peppers. At a fourth-generation family farm, he tries a freshly picked pepper and joins the family for a hearty paprika stew.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol Klein and Diarmuid Gavin, along with guest judge and gardening fan Fred Sirieix, continue the search to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. First up is Anne’s Kilcootry Barn Gardens in Fintona, with two and a half acres of mixed gardens – filled with flower beds, a vegetable garden and an apple orchard. In Omagh, the judges visit a field of dreams – 17 acres of once-barren farmland that’s been transformed by Sir Bob and Lady Rosemary, with a formal Italianate area and a large woodland, planted with wildlife in mind. Finally, it’s off to the shores of Rossglass beach, overlooking Dundrum Bay, to visit artist Bernard’s romantic rose garden. Judges Diarmuid, Carol and Fred are searching for a garden which the owners have put their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide deserves to be the last contender for the title of Greatest Garden?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Can a sweet treat ever be good for you? Zoe Ball is in Birmingham, trying to tempt fitness fanatic Bilal away from sugary sweets and towards flavanol-rich dark chocolate. Chocolate may get a bad press, but the science shows that if you go over to the dark side and eat chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa, it could boost your heart health, improve your brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes. A delicious upgrade with serious benefits.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: The quiz show where players must avoid the Impossible answer to stay in the competition. 21 people begin the week, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 question?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Another four contenders take to the black chair, answering questions on their chosen specialist subjects to earn a place in the semi-finals. Clive Myrie hosts an episode where the specialist subjects are Stephen Hawking, the music of Radiohead, Theda Bara and the Richard Sharpe novels of Bernard Cornwell.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Julie and her daughter Ruby had to downsize to their current home after her partner passed away, leaving them with a much smaller space to house all of their belongings. The team have one day to turn the loft space into a usable storage solution and reclaim space in Ruby’s bedroom and playroom.

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury explore the most stunning parts of Cornwall and Devon to learn how Britain's past lives on in fascinating stories and characters.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. Finds at Shugborough Hall include a rare posset pot, a ceramic garden seat, an 18th-century chest, a Faberge brooch and a stay busk, an 18th-century corset, with a poetic message.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey, Michael Portillo arrives in the historic northern city of Eger. At its famous castle, he discovers how, during the 16th century, Ottoman invaders were defeated by a vastly outnumbered Hungarian force. It‘s a victory still celebrated to this day. Nearby, in the foothills of the Bükk Mountains, one of Hungary’s wine-producing areas, Michael takes a tour of a family-owned vineyard and samples the region’s signature wine, Bull’s Blood. Continuing east, his final destination is the Puszta, Hungary's largest national park, where animals have grazed for over 2,000 years. On a vast plain with endless skies, Michael is treated to an impressive display of horsemanship by Hungarian cowboys, called csikós.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts celebrates the biggest and best archaeological discoveries of 2018 from the north of the UK.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Week two of Springwatch begins with another packed line-up of wildlife stories and live camera action from across the UK. Presenters Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan uncover the dramatic moments captured over the weekend by the live cameras at National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland. This programme explores the remarkable adaptations of some of Britain’s best-loved woodland birds, whilst Iolo Williams heads into the heart of London to reveal the unexpected wildlife thriving in the capital.

programme.title: Emma Barnett: Fighting Endometriosis
programme.desc: Emma Barnett shares her life-long struggle with endometriosis – a disease affecting one in ten women of reproductive age, which causes excruciating, often debilitating, pain. Inside Emma’s body, rogue cells similar to the lining of the womb mysteriously appear on other organs, creating lesions which bleed, scar and cause inflammation. There is no cure. Endometriosis is as common as diabetes but receives a fraction of the research funding. Emma travels across the UK to meet a few of the millions of women who, like her, are suffering. Through candid conversations, they reveal the extent to which endometriosis is devastating the lives of women – affecting fertility, work and loved ones.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to Dorset to fish the River Frome in search of grayling. They last fished for grayling successfully, but this time they have found a stretch of river known for setting the British record – so hopes are high that they could catch something big. As the pair fish along this unspoilt stretch of river, they realise grayling aren't the only species roaming these waters. And later, they attempt mackerel fishing on Chesil Beach, close to the species' feeding peak at dusk.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: In 2010, poorly 16-year-old Megan Bhari decides to launch a charity for seriously ill children. She’s battling a brain tumour, and knowing the toll illness can take, she decides to devote her life to creating fairy-tale experiences that might help children to forget, even briefly, that they’re sick. Believe in Magic becomes an immediate success. Inspired by Megan’s courage and self-sacrifice, celebrities and her beloved One Direction are more than willing to donate their time and money to the cause. And when her mother, Jean, posts news that Megan’s health has deteriorated and she needs expensive treatment in America, countless families and celebrity supporters rally around to support Meg. Together, they raise the money needed overnight. However, in an online chat, some parents whose own children have been treated for cancer abroad begin asking questions.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo explores the stunning Dee valley, from wintering birds along the estuary to displaying black grouse on moorland, rare freshwater-pearl mussels and spawning sea trout.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. As spring gives way to summer, wildlife is at its most active and visitor numbers rise, but wildfires pose a growing threat. They meet the people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK’s seaweed farming industry.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Six months after the race, all five teams reunite to relive their 12,000km journey from Palermo to Mongolia, with unseen moments and unprecedented behind-the-scenes access

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Springwatch returns for three weeks of seasonal splendour. Broadcasting live from the brand new location of National Trust Crom in Northern Ireland, Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan deliver all the wildlife drama spring has to offer. With remote cameras rigged across the site, they uncover stories unfolding in real time. Iolo Williams undertakes his own wildlife wanderings, broadcasting live from the Knepp Estate in Sussex, where wildlife is thriving and ‘stand-out’ species are returning to the landscape.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A hundred years ago in Yorkshire, it wasn’t ketchup that people added to their food - it was a delicious locally made relish. By the turn of the 20th century, 30 million bottles of Yorkshire Relish were being made in Leeds each year and shipped around the world. Made of vinegar, sugar, fruits and 27 spices, it was the go-to sauce for generations, right up until the 1950s. In the city where it was made, Danny Sebastian savours a collection of advertising items that celebrate this once-popular condiment. If that doesn’t whet your appetite, enjoy two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the antiques stalls at Wetherby Racecourse. On the red team are twins and classic car fans Mike and Joe, who hope to breakdance their way to victory - as long as they don’t break the bank first! Against them are mum and daughter Sue and Sarah, who plan on using their cha-cha-charm to get a good price. But which team will be dancing all the way to a profit at Caroline Hawley’s auction?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey, Michael Portillo arrives in the historic northern city of Eger. At its famous castle, he discovers how, during the 16th century, Ottoman invaders were defeated by a vastly outnumbered Hungarian force. It‘s a victory still celebrated to this day. Nearby, in the foothills of the Bükk Mountains, one of Hungary’s wine-producing areas, Michael takes a tour of a family-owned vineyard and samples the region’s signature wine, Bull’s Blood. Continuing east, his final destination is the Puszta, Hungary's largest national park, where animals have grazed for over 2,000 years. On a vast plain with endless skies, Michael is treated to an impressive display of horsemanship by Hungarian cowboys, called csikós.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Leicestershire, Brandy, an elderly horse, is trapped in freezing mud on the banks of the Ashby canal. As the exhausted animal weakens, farmers and a specialist fire and rescue team join forces in a race against the clock to save him. With age and size working against him, the team make one final, desperate attempt to bring Brandy to safety, before it’s too late. In Lincolnshire, arson rips through a family farm, destroying buildings, animal enclosures and years of hard work. As the owners count the cost of the devastation, another farmer in Scotland recounts her own ordeal - watching her only tractor explode into flames after a petrol bomb attack. With deliberate farm fires on the rise, experts reveal how even a single act of recklessness can cause hundreds of thousands of pounds in damage. And in Hampshire, reporter Paul Colgan meets the innovators using drones to deliver defibrillators to the most remote corners of the countryside. By slashing emergency response times, this cutting-edge technology could transform the chances of survival and save lives on farms and in rural communities across the UK.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists join forces with the freedivers to find not one but two family heirlooms lost by a heartbroken daughter. Glass artist Peter turns old, thrown-away bottles into stained-glass beauty, and archaeologist Sam looks into what could be our oldest find by our youngest treasure hunter.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: The quiz show where players must avoid Impossible answers to stay in the competition. 20 people start today’s show, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 question?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock. The subjects in this episode are Marcus Trescothick, Rupert Brooke, Thomas Cromwell and the films of Wes Anderson.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Jan has lived in her three-bedroom house for over 30 years, but working seven days a week leaves her little time to clean and organise, and her collection of clothes and shoes are covered in dust. The team has just one day to turn her bedroom into a relaxing sanctuary and give her space to rediscover her love of fashion.

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury venture across Britain's biggest county, Yorkshire, from the Moors and the Dales to the coast to discover how Britain's epic past lives on in the most fascinating stories and characters. Hugh takes the controls of a glider, and gets winched down into one of Britain's largest potholes on the end of a rope, past the tallest unbroken waterfall in Britain. Julia finds out how Yorkshire's rocks have turned Agatha Christie's handbag to stone, and how they produce mineral water. For a country the size of Britain, it has the biggest variety of geology on Earth, which has created not only a beautiful landscape, but also a fascinating industrial heritage, a rich history and many legends.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show in which members of the public are invited to bring along their antiques for examination. This edition from Ramsgate in Kent features two teddy bears with their own x-ray plates from a dentist, a penny with a date which makes it worth a small fortune, a handbag made from an armadillo shell, and a valuable collection of Louis Wain cat pictures.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from the historic Blackpool Tower Circus. Paul Martin is joined by experts Anita Manning and James Lewis. Together the team pick out a selection of interesting antiques and collectibles to be sold at a local auction. James discovers some terracotta tomb figures that could be over 1,000 years old, and Anita gets her hands on a collection of seaside postcards. But the real star of the show is a well-preserved concertina. Paul also takes a look at one of Lancashire's most famous theatres, the Morecambe Winter Gardens, and tries his hand at some watercolour painting with renowned artist Geoff Butterworth.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Nikki Fox, Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Ignacio Lopez take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multibillion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Just outside the city, Michael boards the Dendermonde to Puurs Heritage Railway, run by volunteers, and gets a chance to drive one of its 1950s diesel engines. Heading to Belgium’s third city, Ghent, Michael visits the magnificent cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb, the most stolen painting in the world.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: It’s fair to say that when it comes to classic national dishes, the French and British like to do things differently. However, both have a passion for poisson. Forty-five per cent of French households eat fish at least once a week, and Marcus wants to find out what kind of fish they like and if there’s a French dish to rival the great British fish and chips. If there's one fruit that might be even more synonymous with Provence than garlic, it's the olive. They’ve been grown here for over 3,500 years, and Marcus wants to understand the region’s passion for these tiny, versatile delights.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the latest seasonal wildlife stories live from National Trust Crom, the stunning lakeside reserve cared for by the National Trust. Featuring part one of a special trilogy of short films examining the extraordinary relationship between the reed warbler and the common cuckoo, one of nature’s most fascinating examples of brood parasitism. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams broadcasts live from the Natural History Museum in London, where he turns the spotlight on the capital’s rich and often-overlooked invertebrate life.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Let Sandi Toksvig bring you to your senses with Aisling Bea, Roisin Conaty, Jessica Fostekew and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The brilliant Ardal O’Hanlon introduces to the stage the amazing Mawaan Rizwan and the hilarious Sara Barron.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
programme.desc: The BBC’s Fergal Keane has spent 40 years witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. So why is he full of hope? The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he’s encountered in the worst places. In this powerful and humane film by Oscar-winner Alice Doyard, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present we have real cause for hope.

programme.title: Defiance
programme.desc: Thriller based on a true story. In 1940s eastern Europe, four Jewish brothers flee to the forest to escape persecution and death at the hands of Nazi forces after their parents are murdered. Once there, they find more refugees are using the forest as a hideout, so they band together to share resources and attempt to outwit the German forces, who are always on their tail.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When a chicken ends up inside Shaun’s pillowcase, Shaun tries to get it back, only for the chicken to end up in the farmhouse and the Farmer to think he is being haunted.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold become reluctant parents when it appears the Baron has wiped his own memory.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: When Pieface rescues Walter and Bertie from danger, Dennis leads the town in celebrating Pieface Day in his honour, and refuses to listen when Pieface tries to tell him it was all one big mistake. Walter uncovers the truth, proclaims himself the bigger hero, and declares it Walter Week!

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Cheese and Dog are teasing each other about the things they like to do. However, trying out each other's interests causes them to realise what they’ve been missing out on.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton tells Patrick he is his conscience.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy pretends to have a twisted ankle so that the Lemmings have to take care of him.

programme.title: Sketchy Comedy
programme.desc: A light switch, a fortune cookie, traffic lights and a bottle are among the items getting the Sketchy treatment!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have one simple job: press buttons in a lift - and they can’t even do that right. Can they get the mayor to the ground floor in time for a grand opening?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When looking after their neighbours' baby, Duck soon learns that he is a terrible babysitter - though it seems kids love Frog! Duck needs Frog to help out for once but soon loses the baby.

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: Zaxos is back and trying everything she can to get into Max and Gark’s good books.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Big Manny joins for an icy challenge, and it’s time to jump in with both feet as Callum sets Adam and Joe an extreme puzzle challenge 15,000 feet in the air!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Blue Peter goes access all areas at Radio 1's Big Weekend with our lucky competition winner! Joel, Abby and Shini try DIY festival make-up looks, and The Adventures of Rap Kid by MC Grammar is the latest read in the Blue Peter Book Club.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gladiator Dynamite sets Dr Dan and Dr Raph a skipping task in Body Battles - who will end up in the ice bath? And in the lab, we find out if we really lose most heat through our heads.

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: As tensions at Orbit HQ reach breaking point, Tilda must outsmart her mum to help Ginny’s family to escape, while Ginny fights for the life she wants on Earth.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Webbthorpe Town Wanderers are in the local cup football match, and Danny and Mick are determined to listen - while they are working, of course. However, the leaky ceiling that they should have fixed is making it difficult to hear the match. Can they fix it, or will they have to fudge it?

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: It’s Danny’s turn to clean the gutters. Unfortunately, Danny is not too keen, and turns up in a full-body plaster cast in order to get out of the work. However, Lance is wise to Danny's tricks and is determined to show Joy that his injuries are just pretend!

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Celebs Viggo Venn and Phil Green come to the aid of Adam and Joe as they try to prove to Callum who is the most talented. With impressions and dad dancing, what’s not to like?

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: Dragons, dinos and divas! The Kwapongs craft fire-breathers, Romesh Ranganathan draws a rapping T-rex, and Bristles dives into the bold, brilliant world of Frida Kahlo.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Emily finds a staggering fact that could spell the end of A-troupe's chances at nationals and the end of one dancer's spot on the team. Fearful of jeopardizing her friendship with Amy, Piper attempts to keep her feelings secret, but Finn uncovers what she has been trying to hide. Meanwhile, Henry learns a hard lesson about honesty after he reveals his true feelings about Kingston's latest news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Zambia for the first time. He hears hippo communication on a whole new level, spots a high-diving croc and meets some incredible sniffer dogs.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special episode of the historical sketch show, focusing on the great British queen Boudica, starring Lorna Watson from Watson & Oliver. We follow Boudica as she turns against her Roman masters and unleashes her wrecking ball on Colchester, St Albans and London. It is cut-throat Celts versus rotten Romans - it is going to be very gory! Meanwhile, across the world, we meet famous emperors Caligula and Nero dishing out their own unique brand of imperial nastiness. With of course, our host Rattus to guide the way!

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: The Real Mutants assemble, people are drinking vomit and electrocuting themselves for science, and the earth and moon have an earworm about the solar system.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper keeps her distance from Finn after he learns her secret. However, when A-troupe's next adventure arrives, Piper and Finn are stuck together in a group dance challenge. Summer struggles to find her place in TNS's plans for their future, until Cleo divulges a different plan of her own. And Miss Angela returns to TNS and engages in a battle with Nick.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob doesn't need a licence to operate a self-driving car!

programme.desc: Jim feels left out when the sardines throw a party to which he is not invited.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: When the last page of the story-time book is lost, Gwen steps in to create the ending.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: Beth shares her slime recipe with her friends, and together they create a goo monster.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog takes advantage of Duck's job at the observatory to watch a football game with the giant telescope - but first he has to sneak in and take control!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck must pick up leaves from the park, but Frog's arrival makes it seem impossible. That is, until Frog comes up with a clever solution - using springs from Duck’s beloved chair!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog get a job in a restaurant kitchen, Frog accidentally makes the soup incredibly spicy. He wants to serve it to a celebrity chef, but Duck wants to stop him!

programme.title: Gifted
programme.desc: Iain leads the search for the missing 209ers, while Ken, Jack and Amanda try to save themselves. Meanwhile, Madame’s hunt for Margery puts her in danger.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: New pitch, new coach, new rivals... No Jack. Jamie is haunted by Jack’s absence from his life but determined to make the best of Phoenix FC’s new circumstances. Advice from Mesut Özil in a fantasy sequence helps firm up his resolve. But although playing in a real stadium at Goodfield Park, home of non-league club Northport Rovers, sounds really cool, Jamie’s less impressed by the threadbare playing surface and their run-down training pitches.

programme.title: The Lady Grace Mysteries
programme.desc: It’s Valentine’s Day, and love letters are flying around. However, Grace only has eyes for the traitor at court - could a mysterious message be the clue she has been searching for?

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: Sports Day kicks off like never before - dance v performance v musical theatre v acting v commercial music... let the races begin!

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: The pressure is on at the end of the season! Can Riley, Maddie Carter and Alex keep their places at the Football Academy for another year?

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe take on a Mesozoic mission when Little Bro Callum challenges them to live as dinosaurs for a day. Can they complete their chores while avoiding the erupting volcano?

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: There is fun and mayhem when Josh is locked out and the DG becomes an adult-free zone! A surprise visitor tests Harmony’s loyalties, and Vinny turns detective to investigate a toilet crime.

programme.title: Armorsaurs
programme.desc: TJ learns teamwork as he directs the team's mission.

programme.title: Blue Peter

programme.desc: Word gets out that the best pizza is to be had from the Mossy Bottom Takeaway.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold takes a trip inside Danger Mouse's body to save him from a deadly aversion to his own heroism.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Hoofius has decided not to quest. Not because he would rather be doing a puzzle or playing his panpipes (though that is rather enjoyable), but because this will be his 1,000th quest and will be added to the Scroll of Honour for all to see for eternity. His friends reluctantly quest without him, but when they don’t return, Hoofius soon comes to regret staying behind.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Boy is afraid of being honest in case he hurts other people's feelings and finds himself doing things he doesn’t like just to keep others happy. Mouse and Cheese encourage him to be totally honest, only to find that Boy’s total honesty can be rather blunt. Can the gang rein him in before Boy alienates the entire town?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this special episode of Deadly 60, Steve Backshall takes a deep dive into the fate of one of the most extraordinary, and endangered, ecosystems on our planet: coral reefs. They may cover less than one per cent of the ocean floor, but they support roughly a quarter of all marine life and are irreplaceable - yet they are in serious trouble. Steve travels to some of the world’s most celebrated reef systems, from the shallows of the Java Sea to the tropical waters of the Maldives. Along the way, he meets all the weird and wonderful creatures that call the reef home, including some of the best-camouflaged creatures and some impressive apex predators - he even gets a telling-off from a clownfish!
